channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [BALRAMCHIN*] Balrampur Chini to raise ₹650 Cr via equity & NCDs; PLA project CAPEX up by ₹230 Cr
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Balrampur Chini Mills Ltd's Board approved raising funds totaling ₹650 crore. This includes a preferential issue of up to 93,16,771 equity shares at ₹483 each, aggregating to ₹450 crore, to promoters and non-promoter institutional investors. Additionally, the board approved issuing up to ₹200 crore in secured, non-convertible debentures via private placement. An Extra-Ordinary General Meeting is scheduled for May 20, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for the preferential issue. The board also revised the capital outlay for its 80,000 TPA Poly Lactic Acid (PLA) Project, increasing it from ₹2,850 crore to ₹3,080 crore, an escalation of ₹230 crore, primarily due to rising material costs and supply chain disruptions.

  • Board approved raising ₹450 Cr via preferential issue of 93.16 lakh equity shares.
  • Promoters and institutional investors will subscribe to the preferential issue.
  • Company to raise an additional ₹200 Cr through private placement of NCDs.
  • EGM on May 20, 2026, for shareholder approval of preferential issue.
  • PLA Project CAPEX revised upwards by ₹230 Cr to ₹3,080 Cr.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS LTD. | Category: Investment

item.title: [BALRAMCHIN*] Balrampur Chini to raise ₹450 Cr via preferential issue & ₹200 Cr via NCDs
item.description:
Balrampur Chini Mills Ltd's Board approved a preferential issue of up to 93,16,771 equity shares at ₹483 each, raising approximately ₹450 crore. Allottees include promoters, promoter group entities, and non-promoter institutional investors like TATA Small Cap Fund and various AIFs. The company will also issue listed, secured, non-convertible debentures (NCDs) aggregating up to ₹200 crore on a private placement basis. An Extra-Ordinary General Meeting is scheduled for May 20, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for the preferential issue. Additionally, the estimated capital outlay for the 80,000 TPA Poly Lactic Acid (PLA) Project has been revised upwards from ₹2,850 crore to ₹3,080 crore, an increase of ₹230 crore, due to cost escalations and design changes.

  • Board approved preferential issue of equity shares worth approximately ₹450 crore.
  • Company to issue listed, secured NCDs aggregating up to ₹200 crore via private placement.
  • EGM scheduled for May 20, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for the preferential issue.
  • PLA Project CAPEX revised from ₹2,850 crore to ₹3,080 crore due to cost escalations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [SFL] Sheela Foam: Sells 575 fractional shares from KEL merger
item.description:
Sheela Foam Ltd. (SFL) announced the sale of 575 fractional shares in the open market. These shares originated from fractional entitlements arising from the share exchange ratio during the amalgamation of Kurlon Enterprise Limited (KEL) with SFL, as approved by the Hon’ble National Company Law Tribunal, Mumbai Bench. A Trust, appointed by SFL's Board, consolidated these fractional entitlements and sold the resulting shares on April 22, 2026. The net proceeds from this sale, after deducting applicable charges and taxes, will be distributed proportionally to the eligible shareholders of Kurlon Enterprise Limited who held these fractional entitlements. This action completes a procedural step in the merger process, ensuring fair compensation for fractional shareholders.

  • 575 fractional shares from KEL merger sold in open market.
  • A Trust consolidated and sold shares on April 22, 2026.
  • Net proceeds will be distributed to eligible KEL shareholders.
  • This completes a procedural step in the KEL-SFL merger.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Sheela Foam Ltd | Category: Merger

item.title: [BALRAMCHIN*] Balrampur Chini Mills to raise ₹450 Cr via Preferential Issue; approves ₹200 Cr NCDs
item.description:
Balrampur Chini Mills Ltd's Board has approved a preferential issue of up to 93,16,771 equity shares at ₹483 each, aggregating to ₹450 crore. The shares will be allotted to promoters, promoter group entities, and non-promoter institutional investors including TATA Small Cap Fund and various AIFs. An Extra-Ordinary General Meeting is scheduled for May 20, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for this issue. Additionally, the Board approved the issuance of Listed, Secured, Non-Convertible Debentures aggregating up to ₹200 crore on a private placement basis. The company also revised the estimated capital outlay for its 80,000 TPA Poly Lactic Acid (PLA) Project from ₹2,850 crore to ₹3,080 crore, an increase of ₹230 crore, primarily due to cost escalations and design changes.

  • Company to raise ₹450 crore through preferential issue of 9.32 million equity shares.
  • Shares priced at ₹483 each, allotted to promoters and institutional investors.
  • Board also approved raising up to ₹200 crore via secured Non-Convertible Debentures.
  • CAPEX for the PLA Project revised upwards by ₹230 crore to ₹3,080 crore.
  • Shareholder approval for preferential issue to be sought at May 20, 2026 EGM.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [MITSU] Mitsu Chem Plast Board to meet May 2 for FY26 results & dividend
item.description:
Mitsu Chem Plast Ltd has announced that its Board of Directors will convene on Saturday, May 02, 2026. The primary agenda includes considering and approving the Audited Financial Results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. Additionally, the Board will consider and recommend a final dividend, if any, on equity shares for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, for approval at the ensuing 38th Annual General Meeting. In compliance with insider trading regulations, the trading window for designated persons has been closed from April 1, 2026, and will remain closed until 48 hours after the declaration of the financial results.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 2, 2026.
  • To consider Q4 and FY26 audited financial results.
  • Final dividend recommendation for FY26 to be discussed.
  • Trading window closed for designated persons until results declaration.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Mitsu Chem Plast Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [MITSU] Mitsu Chem Plast Board to meet May 2 for FY26 results & dividend
item.description:
Mitsu Chem Plast Ltd has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on May 02, 2026. The primary agenda includes considering and approving the Audited Financial Results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. Additionally, the board will consider and recommend a final dividend, if any, on equity shares for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, for shareholder approval at the 38th Annual General Meeting. In compliance with insider trading regulations, the trading window for designated persons has been closed from April 1, 2026, and will remain closed until 48 hours after the declaration of the financial results.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 02, 2026.
  • To consider and approve Audited Financial Results for Q4 and FY26.
  • To consider and recommend a final dividend for FY26.
  • Trading window closed from April 1, 2026, until results declaration.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Mitsu Chem Plast Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [LUXIND] Lux Industries Board approves demerger plan following family settlement
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Lux Industries Limited's Board of Directors was informed about a Family Settlement Agreement (FSA) among the Todi Family, who are the company's promoters. The company itself is not a party to this FSA. Following this, the Board granted in-principle approval for a scheme of demerger. This plan involves trifurcating the company's business into three verticals (A, B, and C). Verticals A and C are proposed to be demerged into two new, subsequently listed companies, while Vertical B will remain with Lux Industries. Leadership for each resulting entity will be assigned to different branches of the Todi family. The AKT Family and KKT Family will relinquish management and control rights in Lux Industries, with the PKT Family retaining control. To facilitate the demerger, the Board also approved the immediate incorporation of two new wholly-owned subsidiaries. Additionally, a revised brand licensing agreement with Biswanath Hosiery Mills Limited for 'Lux' related brands was approved.

  • Promoter Todi Family entered a Family Settlement Agreement (FSA).
  • Board approved in-principle demerger into three separate listed entities.
  • Verticals A and C to demerge; Vertical B remains with Lux Industries.
  • Leadership roles assigned to different Todi family branches.
  • Two new wholly-owned subsidiaries to be incorporated for demerger.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Lux Industries Ltd | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [LUXIND] Lux Industries Board Approves In-Principle Demerger & Family Settlement
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Lux Industries Ltd's Board of Directors, in a meeting on April 23, 2026, informed about a Family Settlement Agreement (FSA) among the promoter Todi Family. Pursuant to the FSA, the Board granted in-principle approval for a scheme of demerger. This scheme will trifurcate the company's business into three verticals (A, B, C). Verticals A and C will be demerged into two new, subsequently listed companies, led by the AKT Family and KKT Family respectively. Vertical B will remain with Lux Industries, led by the PKT Family. The Board also approved the immediate incorporation of two new wholly-owned subsidiaries to facilitate the demerger and a revised brand licensing agreement with Biswanath Hosiery Mills Limited.

  • Board gives in-principle approval for a scheme of demerger.
  • Demerger follows a Family Settlement Agreement among promoters.
  • Business to be trifurcated; two verticals to be demerged and listed.
  • Two new wholly-owned subsidiaries to be incorporated for demerger.
  • Revised brand licensing agreement approved to safeguard brand rights.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Lux Industries Ltd | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [PACEDIGITK] Pace Digitek signs exclusive OEM deal with NEC XON for Africa
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Pace Digitek Ltd has executed an Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) Exclusivity Agreement with NEC XON Systems Proprietary Limited. This agreement, signed on April 22, 2026, grants NEC XON exclusive rights to market, distribute, and sell Pace Digitek's products, including Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and other related energy solutions. The exclusive territories covered by this international partnership are South Africa, Botswana, Mozambique, Namibia, and Mauritius. The company clarified that NEC XON is an international entity, and the agreement does not involve any related party transactions or shareholding.

  • Pace Digitek signed an exclusive OEM agreement with NEC XON Systems.
  • NEC XON gains exclusive rights to market BESS and energy solutions.
  • The partnership covers South Africa, Botswana, Mozambique, Namibia, and Mauritius.
  • This expands Pace Digitek's market reach into key African territories.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Pace Digitek Ltd | Category: Partnership

item.title: [EMBASSY] Embassy Office Parks REIT's Crisil AAA/Stable Rating Reaffirmed
item.description:
Embassy Office Parks REIT has announced the annual review of its credit ratings by Crisil Ratings Limited. Crisil has reaffirmed its 'Crisil AAA/Stable' rating for various Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) and 'Crisil A1+' for Commercial Papers (CPs) issued or to be issued by the REIT. The 'Crisil AAA/Stable' rating indicates the highest degree of safety regarding timely servicing of debt obligations and the lowest credit risk. The 'Crisil A1+' rating signifies very strong capacity for timely payment of financial obligations. All ratings were reaffirmed on April 22, 2026, and are subject to continuous surveillance by Crisil.

  • Crisil reaffirmed 'Crisil AAA/Stable' rating for Embassy REIT's NCDs.
  • Crisil reaffirmed 'Crisil A1+' rating for Embassy REIT's Commercial Papers.
  • 'Crisil AAA/Stable' indicates highest safety and lowest credit risk.
  • All credit ratings were reaffirmed on April 22, 2026.
  • Ratings are under continuous surveillance and review by Crisil.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Embassy Office Parks REIT | Category: Rating
